title: The Great Victory of Bach Dang 1288
summary: In 1288, Hung Dao Vuong Tran Quoc Tuan repeated the miracle on the Bach Dang River, crushing the powerful Mongol-Yuan army and securing national sovereignty.

slides.text: The Mongol-Yuan army invaded our country for the third time with an unprecedentedly large naval force.
slides.imagePrompt: Large Mongol fleet with many ships sailing into Vietnam, powerful and threatening, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Tran Hung Dao inherited Ngo Quyen's strategy, ordering the placement of wooden stakes in the Bach Dang River to prepare for the enemy.
slides.imagePrompt: General Tran Hung Dao pointing at the river, soldiers hammering iron headed wooden stakes into the river bed at low tide, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: The battle was fierce. Our troops feigned a retreat to lure the enemy ships into the stake field while the tide was receding.
slides.imagePrompt: Vietnamese small agile boats leading large Mongol ships towards the hidden stakes, river water level dropping, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Enemy ships were pierced by the stakes and shattered. Tran Hung Dao commanded our troops in a powerful and valiant counter-attack.
slides.imagePrompt: Mongol ships crashing on wooden stakes, soldiers falling, Tran Hung Dao's army attacking from both sides, heroic victory scene, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.question: Which general commanded the 1288 victory on the Bach Dang River?
What powerful force did the Mongol-Yuan army bring when invading for the third time?
